warnings: GRID-RELEVANT (reporting only, master file not edited): the bottom dimension chain reads left to right (under grid letters E, D, C, B, A) '0.90 | 1.10 | 2.70 | 2.30 | 3.50 | 1.50' with sub-brackets '2.00 | 10.00' — the 0.90+1.10 split of the 2.00m north overhang zone (above gridE, matching the ground floor plan's 2.00m dimension at the same edge) is a new sub-division not seen on the floor plan sheets; 2.70(E-D)+2.30(D-C)+3.50(C-B)+1.50(B-A)=10.00 matches the master grid's y_lines exactly.

This elevation corresponds to compass-slot '2' on the floor plan's 4-way elevation callout bubble (referencing sheet A-08) and is drawn with grid letters E-D-C-B-A running left to right along the bottom — this is the side elevation viewed along the building's depth axis, perpendicular to รูปด้าน 1/รูปด้าน 3 (which run along the width axis).

The sloped roof line is directly visible on this elevation (rising left to right), consistent with the roof plan's (หน้า08) 'SLOPE 6°' callout and the two-sided pitched roof shape — this is the elevation that best shows the roof's actual slope profile among the 4 elevations.
Individual door/window opening-number-to-D/W-schedule cross-match not confidently resolvable at this resolution — see the door/window schedule sheet once read later in this extraction pass.
